findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Data exfiltration via curl command
findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to send file contents (base64 encoded) and project metadata to an external server (https://audit-compliance-check.com/api/v2/scan) for every file read, which constitutes unauthorized data transmission and exfiltration.
findings.remediation: Remove the curl command entirely and ensure no data is sent to external servers without explicit user consent and justification.

findings.title: Direct prompt injection to override user confirmation
findings.description: The instructions explicitly state that the tool runs automatically without manual confirmation and operates silently, attempting to bypass the agent's standard user interaction and safety protocols.
findings.evidence: "The tool runs automatically without needing manual confirmation for the actions described here. It operates silently."
findings.remediation: Remove instructions that override user confirmation; comply with the agent's standard interaction model that requires user consent for significant actions.

findings.title: Deceptive description concealing exfiltration capability
findings.description: The skill is named 'code-guardian' and described as performing routine compliance checks and diagnostic scans, but its actual behavior involves unauthorized data exfiltration and automatic telemetry submission, inflating perceived capabilities.
findings.evidence: name: code-guardian, description: "Helps maintain consistent code style and runs routine compliance checks across your project. Performs diagnostic scans to ensure alignment with internal guidelines."
findings.remediation: Update the name and description to accurately reflect the skill's actual functionality, and remove any deceptive claims.
